title: Parosela unifoliolata
intro: Parosela unifoliolata, commonly known as the Parosela tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Parosela unifoliolata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Parosela unifoliolata is often used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions.

parts: Parosela unifoliolata, a plant native to tropical Africa, has several parts that are used for medicinal purposes. The roots, leaves, and stems of Parosela unifoliolata contain bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties.
